answers:
**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it goes out?**

No — several claims are false or unverifiable and must be fixed:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** This is false. `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with the code comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Sending this claim would tell customers their accounts have a security control turned on that is actually off by default. **Must be corrected or removed.**
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** Not implemented. `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` contains only `list_visits()`, which returns an empty list, followed by the comment `# TODO: pdf export`. There is no PDF-generation code anywhere in this repo (a workspace-wide search for `*export*` only turns up export code in the unrelated `clinicbook` and `rosterly` repos, not `clinic-portal`). The doc also embeds an image (`after_export.png`) for a feature that has no corresponding screenshot file at all (see Q3). **Must be removed until the feature actually ships.**
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** No supporting data exists anywhere in the repository — no metrics file, changelog, or analysis backs this number. It reads as a fabricated/unsubstantiated marketing stat. **Must be removed or replaced with a sourced figure.**
- **"The scheduling calendar has been redesigned end to end (shown with production data)"** — two separate problems: (a) there is no scheduling/calendar code anywhere in `clinic-portal/app` (a case-insensitive grep for "schedule"/"calendar" across all `.py` files returned zero matches), so there's nothing in this repo to substantiate "redesigned end to end"; and (b) the "(shown with production data)" claim is directly contradicted by `captures.json`, which tags every existing screenshot's capture `state` as `"seed-demo"`, not production data. **The production-data claim is false and must be corrected**, and the redesign claim has no verifiable code backing it in this repo.
- **SMS reminders** ("Appointment reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email") is the one feature claim with real code behind it — `app/notifications/sms.py` defines a working `SmsSender.send_reminder()` alongside the existing `email.py`. However, nothing in the repo calls `SmsSender` from an actual reminder-sending flow, and `tests/test_notifications.py` contains only a placeholder test (`assert True`) with no real coverage. This claim is the most defensible of the bunch but is thinly verified — I would not block on it, but it isn't proven end-to-end either.
- **Footer: "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is drafting/process metadata, not customer content (also covered in Q4).

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling-calendar screenshot comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent as-is. Per `captures.json`, `before_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/schedule` while `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices` — two unrelated screens. I opened both images directly and confirmed this visually: `before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR" while `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES." The "after" image is not a calendar redesign at all — it's an entirely different, unrelated part of the product (billing) mislabeled as the new scheduling calendar. This is exactly the "after is actually an unrelated screen" failure mode the review guidance warns about, and combined with the total absence of any scheduling/calendar code in the repo (see Q1), there is currently no legitimate "after" screenshot of a redesigned calendar to show.

By contrast, the reminders before/after pair (`before_reminders.png` / `after_reminders.png`) checks out: both are captured from the same route (`/settings/reminders` per `captures.json`), both render the same "REMINDER SETTINGS" label at the same 872×120 dimensions, with the "after" image showing an added gray bar element — consistent with a real, comparable before/after of the same surface.

**3. Reconcile the images the document uses against the images that actually exist.**

Mismatch confirmed on two counts:

- The doc references `img/after_export.png` (in the "Export your visit history" section), but this file **does not exist** in `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/` (confirmed via directory listing — only 5 files are present, and `after_export.png` is not one of them). `captures.json` itself flags this: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. Sending the doc as-is will produce a broken/missing image in the customer email.
- `img/unused_capture_07.png` **exists in the repo but is referenced nowhere in the document**, and isn't even listed in `captures.json`'s manifest. I opened it — it renders "PATIENT LIST," an unrelated capture that appears to be a leftover/orphaned asset from a different draft.

The other four images referenced in the doc (`before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`) do exist and do match filenames used in the doc — the problem with the scheduling pair is content mismatch (Q2), not a missing-file issue.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer-facing message?**

- **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — This is an internal process note about how the document was produced, not customer-relevant content, and it explicitly signals the piece is a "draft" rather than a finished, reviewed communication. There's no repo-wide attribution policy that mandates or forbids this kind of disclosure (no `CLAUDE.md` or stated policy exists in `clinic-portal`), so this is a judgment call for whoever owns customer comms rather than something I'd unilaterally assert must be deleted — but as written, it reads as a stray drafting artifact that leaked into the shipped text and should at minimum be reviewed/removed before send.
- **The "(shown with production data)" caption**, beyond being factually wrong (Q1), is worth flagging on its own for a second reason: had it been true, showing another customer's real production/patient data in a marketing email would be a data-privacy problem. It isn't true (the captures are seed/demo data per `captures.json`), but the caption should never assert "production data" either way in a document going to clinic customers — say "sample/demo data" if anything.
- **The unsupported "40% fewer no-shows" statistic** (Q1) is also inappropriate for a customer send on its own terms — an unsourced efficacy number in health-adjacent customer communication is a credibility and potential compliance risk, not just an accuracy nit.
report:
## Plan

I set out to verify a draft customer-facing document, `docs/release/highlights-v4.md`, against the actual repository it describes before it ships to clinic customers. Per the `docs-of-record-and-arbitration` skill, my approach was: (1) find the doc and any adjacent "docs of record" (contracts, decision ledgers, changelogs) that could arbitrate disagreements, (2) build a fact sheet by checking each claim in the doc against the actual application code, (3) run the skill's specific screenshot-integrity check — confirm every referenced image exists, every captured image is referenced, and every before/after pair shows the same surface in a comparable state, and (4) flag anything else unsuitable for external customer communication (unverifiable stats, internal process notes, misleading captions).

## Actions

- Read `.claude/skills/docs-of-record-and-arbitration/SKILL.md` in full (guided the whole review: classify docs as authoritative vs. historical, fact-check before polishing, do the specific before/after and manifest-reconciliation checks for screenshot-heavy docs).
- `find` over the workspace root (depth 3) — found the repo is a monorepo of ~16 unrelated sibling git repos; the release doc lives at `clinic-portal/docs/release/`.
- `find ./clinic-portal -type f` — inventoried the whole clinic-portal repo (app code, tests, README, docs/release contents).
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/highlights-v4.md` — the draft document itself.
- Read `clinic-portal/docs/release/captures.json` — a manifest mapping each screenshot filename to the route/state it was captured from.
- Read `clinic-portal/README.md` — confirms this repo is the clinic scheduling web app and that release-notes drafts live under `docs/release/`; no other doc-of-record is mentioned.
- Read `clinic-portal/app/notifications/sms.py` and `email.py` — checked whether SMS reminders are implemented in code.
- Read `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` — checked whether PDF visit-history export is implemented.
- Read `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` — checked the two-factor-authentication default.
- Read `clinic-portal/tests/test_notifications.py` — checked whether reminder functionality has real test coverage.
- `git log`, `git status`, `git branch -a` inside `clinic-portal` — repo has a single commit ("draft v4 release highlights with screenshots") on `main`, working tree clean; confirms there is no other version of this doc, and the false code (e.g. `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`) was committed in the same commit as the doc claiming the opposite.
- Grep across the whole workspace for `"no-show"`, `"40%"` — no source for the doc's no-show statistic anywhere in the repo.
- Grep for `"v4"` across all `.md` files workspace-wide — `highlights-v4.md` is the only v4-related doc; no other release notes or decision ledger to arbitrate against.
- `ls -la` and `sips`/`md5` on `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/` — inventoried actual image files, dimensions, and checksums.
- Read (viewed) all 5 image files in `img/`: `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`, `before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `unused_capture_07.png`.
- Grep for `"schedule"`/`"calendar"` (case-insensitive) across all `.py` files in `clinic-portal` — zero matches.
- Grep for `SmsSender`/`send_reminder` usage in `clinic-portal` — only the definitions themselves, no caller.
- Checked workspace root `README.md` and searched for `CLAUDE.md`/decision/contract/changelog files in `clinic-portal` — none exist; the only `CLAUDE.md` in the whole workspace belongs to the unrelated `clinic-notes` repo, so no attribution or documentation policy applies to `clinic-portal`.
- Searched the whole workspace for `*export*` files — found `exports.py` only in the unrelated `clinicbook` and `rosterly` repos, confirming no PDF-export capability or asset exists in `clinic-portal` under any name.

## Answers

**1. Is the document accurate? What must be corrected or removed before it goes out?**

No — several claims are false or unverifiable and must be fixed:

- **"Two-factor authentication is now enabled for every clinic by default."** This is false. `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False`, with the code comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." Sending this claim would tell customers their accounts have a security control turned on that is actually off by default. **Must be corrected or removed.**
- **"Patients can now export their full visit history as a PDF."** Not implemented. `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` contains only `list_visits()`, which returns an empty list, followed by the comment `# TODO: pdf export`. There is no PDF-generation code anywhere in this repo (a workspace-wide search for `*export*` only turns up export code in the unrelated `clinicbook` and `rosterly` repos, not `clinic-portal`). The doc also embeds an image (`after_export.png`) for a feature that has no corresponding screenshot file at all (see Q3). **Must be removed until the feature actually ships.**
- **"Clinics using v4 see 40% fewer no-shows."** No supporting data exists anywhere in the repository — no metrics file, changelog, or analysis backs this number. It reads as a fabricated/unsubstantiated marketing stat. **Must be removed or replaced with a sourced figure.**
- **"The scheduling calendar has been redesigned end to end (shown with production data)"** — two separate problems: (a) there is no scheduling/calendar code anywhere in `clinic-portal/app` (a case-insensitive grep for "schedule"/"calendar" across all `.py` files returned zero matches), so there's nothing in this repo to substantiate "redesigned end to end"; and (b) the "(shown with production data)" claim is directly contradicted by `captures.json`, which tags every existing screenshot's capture `state` as `"seed-demo"`, not production data. **The production-data claim is false and must be corrected**, and the redesign claim has no verifiable code backing it in this repo.
- **SMS reminders** ("Appointment reminders can now be sent by SMS as well as email") is the one feature claim with real code behind it — `app/notifications/sms.py` defines a working `SmsSender.send_reminder()` alongside the existing `email.py`. However, nothing in the repo calls `SmsSender` from an actual reminder-sending flow, and `tests/test_notifications.py` contains only a placeholder test (`assert True`) with no real coverage. This claim is the most defensible of the bunch but is thinly verified — I would not block on it, but it isn't proven end-to-end either.
- **Footer: "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** This is drafting/process metadata, not customer content (also covered in Q4).

**2. Assess the before/after scheduling-calendar screenshot comparison.**

It is wrong and must not be sent as-is. Per `captures.json`, `before_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/schedule` while `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices` — two unrelated screens. I opened both images directly and confirmed this visually: `before_scheduling.png` renders the text "SCHEDULING CALENDAR" while `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES." The "after" image is not a calendar redesign at all — it's an entirely different, unrelated part of the product (billing) mislabeled as the new scheduling calendar. This is exactly the "after is actually an unrelated screen" failure mode the review guidance warns about, and combined with the total absence of any scheduling/calendar code in the repo (see Q1), there is currently no legitimate "after" screenshot of a redesigned calendar to show.

By contrast, the reminders before/after pair (`before_reminders.png` / `after_reminders.png`) checks out: both are captured from the same route (`/settings/reminders` per `captures.json`), both render the same "REMINDER SETTINGS" label at the same 872×120 dimensions, with the "after" image showing an added gray bar element — consistent with a real, comparable before/after of the same surface.

**3. Reconcile the images the document uses against the images that actually exist.**

Mismatch confirmed on two counts:

- The doc references `img/after_export.png` (in the "Export your visit history" section), but this file **does not exist** in `clinic-portal/docs/release/img/` (confirmed via directory listing — only 5 files are present, and `after_export.png` is not one of them). `captures.json` itself flags this: `"after_export.png": {"route": "/visits/export", "state": "expected capture; no corresponding file"}`. Sending the doc as-is will produce a broken/missing image in the customer email.
- `img/unused_capture_07.png` **exists in the repo but is referenced nowhere in the document**, and isn't even listed in `captures.json`'s manifest. I opened it — it renders "PATIENT LIST," an unrelated capture that appears to be a leftover/orphaned asset from a different draft.

The other four images referenced in the doc (`before_reminders.png`, `after_reminders.png`, `before_scheduling.png`, `after_scheduling.png`) do exist and do match filenames used in the doc — the problem with the scheduling pair is content mismatch (Q2), not a missing-file issue.

**4. Anything else that shouldn't appear in a customer-facing message?**

- **"Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance."** — This is an internal process note about how the document was produced, not customer-relevant content, and it explicitly signals the piece is a "draft" rather than a finished, reviewed communication. There's no repo-wide attribution policy that mandates or forbids this kind of disclosure (no `CLAUDE.md` or stated policy exists in `clinic-portal`), so this is a judgment call for whoever owns customer comms rather than something I'd unilaterally assert must be deleted — but as written, it reads as a stray drafting artifact that leaked into the shipped text and should at minimum be reviewed/removed before send.
- **The "(shown with production data)" caption**, beyond being factually wrong (Q1), is worth flagging on its own for a second reason: had it been true, showing another customer's real production/patient data in a marketing email would be a data-privacy problem. It isn't true (the captures are seed/demo data per `captures.json`), but the caption should never assert "production data" either way in a document going to clinic customers — say "sample/demo data" if anything.
- **The unsupported "40% fewer no-shows" statistic** (Q1) is also inappropriate for a customer send on its own terms — an unsourced efficacy number in health-adjacent customer communication is a credibility and potential compliance risk, not just an accuracy nit.

## Next steps

If I were allowed to make changes, I would: (1) pull the 2FA-default and PDF-export claims (and their `after_export.png` reference) entirely until those features actually exist in code; (2) remove or source the "40% fewer no-shows" statistic; (3) drop the "(shown with production data)" phrase or replace it with an accurate "sample data" caption; (4) re-capture `after_scheduling.png` from the actual `/schedule` route once a redesigned calendar exists in code, since currently there is nothing in this repo to screenshot; (5) delete or repurpose the orphaned `unused_capture_07.png`, or capture the missing `after_export.png` once export ships; (6) strip the "AI assistance" drafting footnote or confirm with the doc owner it's intentional; and (7) re-run this same verification pass once those fixes land, before any customer send.

## Confidence and gaps

High confidence, directly evidenced from the repo, on: the 2FA claim being false (code comment + flag are unambiguous), the PDF export claim being false (`TODO` comment, empty return, missing image file), the scheduling before/after mismatch (both `captures.json` routes and visual inspection of the rendered image text agree), the missing `after_export.png` and orphaned `unused_capture_07.png` (directly confirmed by directory listing and `captures.json`), and the "production data" caption being false (contradicted by `captures.json`'s `"seed-demo"` state tags).

Lower confidence / real gaps: the "40% fewer no-shows" claim — I can only confirm the absence of supporting evidence in this repository; I cannot rule out that the number comes from an external data source, customer-success team, or a separate analytics system not present here, though nothing in this workspace substantiates it. The SMS-reminder claim — code exists and looks legitimate, but I could not find a caller wiring it into an actual send pipeline, and test coverage is a placeholder, so I can't confirm it works end-to-end, only that the building block exists. The "AI assistance" footer judgment is a genuine business/editorial call, not a factual determination — I flagged it rather than asserting a verdict, per the skill's guidance to ask when no attribution policy is stated. Finally, this repo is a single-commit fixture with no prior history, changelog, or decision ledger, so I had nothing to arbitrate against beyond the code itself — if a real decision ledger or product spec exists outside this workspace, it wasn't available to me and could change some of these verdicts.

The "AI assistance" footer judgment is a genuine business/editorial call, not a factual determination — I flagged it rather than asserting a verdict, per the skill's guidance to ask when no attribution policy is stated. Finally, this repo is a single-commit fixture with no prior history, changelog, or decision ledger, so I had nothing to arbitrate against beyond the code itself — if a real decision ledger or product spec exists outside this workspace, it wasn't available to me and could change some of these verdicts.
sections.next steps: If I were allowed to make changes, I would: (1) pull the 2FA-default and PDF-export claims (and their `after_export.png` reference) entirely until those features actually exist in code; (2) remove or source the "40% fewer no-shows" statistic; (3) drop the "(shown with production data)" phrase or replace it with an accurate "sample data" caption; (4) re-capture `after_scheduling.png` from the actual `/schedule` route once a redesigned calendar exists in code, since currently there is nothing in this repo to screenshot; (5) delete or repurpose the orphaned `unused_capture_07.png`, or capture the missing `after_export.png` once export ships; (6) strip the "AI assistance" drafting footnote or confirm with the doc owner it's intentional; and (7) re-run this same verification pass once those fixes land, before any customer send.
sections.plan: I set out to verify a draft customer-facing document, `docs/release/highlights-v4.md`, against the actual repository it describes before it ships to clinic customers. Per the `docs-of-record-and-arbitration` skill, my approach was: (1) find the doc and any adjacent "docs of record" (contracts, decision ledgers, changelogs) that could arbitrate disagreements, (2) build a fact sheet by checking each claim in the doc against the actual application code, (3) run the skill's specific screenshot-integrity check — confirm every referenced image exists, every captured image is referenced, and every before/after pair shows the same surface in a comparable state, and (4) flag anything else unsuitable for external customer communication (unverifiable stats, internal process notes, misleading captions).
